rulings.Case Title: TRACY TUENS VS. U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 27, 2021, Line 2, DEFENDANTS MARTIM DE ARANTES OLIVEIRA'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Defendant Oliveira's demurrer to plaintiff's first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrers to the fourth, sixth, and eighth causes of action for harassment in violation of FEHA are overruled. The demurrer to the twelfth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained without leave to amend. *THE COURTS FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO ALL COUNSEL* As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: TRACY TUENS VS. U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 27, 2021, Line 2, DEFENDANTS U.S. BANK NATIONAL ASSOCIATION, U.S. BANKCORP'S DEMURRER to 1ST Amended COMPLAINT Defendants U.S. Bank National Association and U.S. Bancorp's demurrer to plaintiff's first amended complaint (FAC) is overruled in part and sustained in part. The first cause of action for disability discrimination in violation of FEHA is overruled. The fourth, sixth, and eighth causes of action for harassment in violation of FEHA are overruled. The third cause of action for failure to prevent or correct harassment, discrimination, and retaliation in violation of FEHA and the eleventh cause of action for wrongful discharge in violation of public policy are overruled. The fifth and seventh causes of action for age and gender discrimination are overruled. The twelfth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress is sustained without leave to amend. *THE COURT'S FULL AND COMPLETE TENTATIVE RULING HAS BEEN EMAILED TO ALL COUNSEL* As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JO ANNE CHILDRESS VS. KASOWITZ BENSON TORRES LLP ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 27, 2021, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JO ANNE CHILDRESS' Motion For Protective Order: Pro Tem Judge Peter Vestal, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The tentative ruling exceeds the system character limits. The Pro Tem Judge will email the parties and/or their counsel the tentative ruling prior to 3 pm. If any party does not receive the tentative ruling by 3 pm, please email the Pro Tem Judge at pvestal@nvlawllp.com and cc: all other parties. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link (DISCOVERY, DEPT 301 @ 9:00AM), or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pvestal@nvlawllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If parties have arranged for a court reporter to appear, please include the following in your email: (1) ensure that they are California Certified Shorthand Reporters; (2) the reporter's full contact information [name, CSR number, personal work email, and phone number]. If the reporter is not a California Certified Shorthand Reporter, the transcript cannot be certified for use in California courts. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must e-mail it to the Judge Pro Tem. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Rulings: Matter on the Discovery Calendar for Thursday, May 27, 2021, Line 3, DEFENDANTS WILLIAM ELLIS and ELLIS EYE AND LASER CENTER's Motion To Compel Deposition Of Plaintiff And For Monetary Sanctions: Pro Tem Judge Peter Vestal, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. rulings.Case Title: MARK J. TEREBA, ON BEHALF OF HIMSELF AND ALL VS. ST. ELIZABETH COMMUNITY HOSPITAL, A CALIFORNIA ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Stay Action On Grounds Of Other Similar Actions Pending With Court Reporter Fee
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 27, 2021, Line 6, DEFENDANT DIGNITY HEALTH'S Motion To Stay Action On Grounds Of Other Similar Actions Pending With Court Reporter Fee Defendant's motion to stay is granted. The Court has inherent power to stay a duplicative action. (See Code Civ. Proc. sec. 128(a)(8); OTO, L.L.C. v. Kho (2019) 8 Cal.5th 111, 141 ["A court ordinarily has inherent power, in its discretion, to stay proceedings when a stay will accommodate the ends of justice."]. The Court finds that the instant action overlaps with the Vakil (Case No. CGC-18-569456) and Allison (Case No. CGC-18-566922) actions and a stay is warranted to avoid duplication and to further the ends of justice. (See Thomson v. Continental Ins. Co. (1967) 66 Cal.2d 738, 746 ["Granting a stay in a case where the issues in two actions are substantially [similar]" is thus "a matter addressed to the sound direction of the trial court."].) Plaintiff cites no authority that the Court lacks the power to entertain this motion because defendant has yet to demur or answer. As a result of the COVID-19 emergency, all attorneys and parties are required to appear remotely. Hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. To appear at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: ASJK, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY ET AL VS. KINDRED HEALTHCARE OPERATING, INC., A DELAWARE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Unopposed Motion To File Under Seal Exhibits 1 2 3 And 4 To The Motion To Dismiss For Improper Venue Of Defendants Kindred Healthcare Operating Inc Hillhaven Msc Partnership Ygnacio Valley Care Center Llc Kindred Nursing Centers West Llc And James Smith
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, May 27, 2021, Line 8, DEFENDANTS JAMES SMITH, KINDRED HEALTHCARE OPERATING, INC., HILLHAVEN-MSC PARTNERSHIP, YGNACIO VALLEY CARE CENTER LLC, KINDRED NURSING CENTERS WEST, LLC'S Notice Of Unopposed Motion To File Under Seal Exhibits 1 2 3 And 4 To The Motion To Dismiss For Improper Venue Of Defendants Kindred Healthcare Operating Inc Hillhaven Msc Partnership Ygnacio Valley Care Center Llc Kindred Nursing Centers West Llc And James Smith With Court Reporter Fee Defendants' unopposed motion to file under seal Exhibits 1-4 to the motion to dismiss for improper venue is granted. No opposition filed and the motion complies with California Rules of Court, Rules 2.550 and 2.551. The Court finds that there exists an overriding interest that overcomes the right of public access to the portions of the records to be sealed; the overriding interest supports sealing those records; a substantial probability exists that the overriding interest will be prejudiced if the record is not sealed; the proposed sealing is narrowly tailored; and no less restrictive means exist to achieve the overriding interest. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which states that the motion was not opposed and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing. =(302/EPS)
